reforms.description: The Irish government introduced an extensive system of grants and a tax holiday on profits for export-oriented foreign enterprises to accelerate industrialisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The introduction of grants and tax holidays is expected to stimulate investment and innovation in the industrial sector, thereby enhancing economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice as part of the government's strategy to enhance industrialisation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at accelerating industrialisation through financial incentives.
reforms.source_quote: The main incentives offered both to foreign and domestic investors to expand industrial activities are an extensive system of grants and a tax holiday on profits on exports.

reforms.description: The Industrial Development Authority provides grants for research and development projects, covering up to 50% of the project costs, to encourage innovation in manufacturing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing substantial financial support for R&D, the reform aims to foster innovation and competitiveness in the manufacturing sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The grants have been actively provided to support R&D in manufacturing since their introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is more focused and does not represent a systemic overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Industrial Development Act (1969) provides that grants may be approved for research and development projects, up to a maximum of 50% of the cost of each project.
